title: Reconstruct Driver Trajectories And Use Rolling Degradation Slopes
summary: Public F1 tyre-degradation work models latent pace as a dynamic state with pit stops acting as resets, which fits this task better than mostly static row-wise features.
rationale: Public F1 tyre-degradation work models latent pace as a dynamic state with pit stops acting as resets, which fits this task better than mostly static row-wise features. The current strong solutions barely use explicit lagged trajectory information beyond raw current-lap values.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year, Race, Driver)` ordered by `LapNumber`, create lag-1/2/3 features for `LapTime_Delta`, `Position`, `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`; rolling slopes over the last 2-5 laps; post-pit reset indicators; and stint-start normalized quantities like growth in degradation since stint start. Optionally fuel-correct lap time by regressing `LapTime (s)` on `RaceProgress` within race, then use residual trend features.
expected_effect: Should add genuinely new signal about accelerating tyre drop-off and imminent strategy windows, especially for drivers whose raw tyre age alone is ambiguous.
risk: Ordering/reconstruction errors around safety-car-like anomalies or missing laps can inject noise; needs careful group sorting and reset logic.
